programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Jan Di's bike is set ablaze while she is attacked with water balloons and blasted with a fire extinguisher.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: A psychic foresees that Jan Di will be losing something significant.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Jun Pyo intends to expel his best friend and Jan Di out of Shinhwa High School -- and out of F4.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Jan Di dashes to the hospital after being informed that Jun Pyo had been in an accident.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Yi-jung gets a frantic call from his mother threatening to commit suicide because of his father.

programme.title: High School Debut
programme.desc: Yoh agrees to teach Haruna how to get a boyfriend but on the condition she doesn't fall in love with him.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Yi Jung's mother frantically calls him threatening to commit suicide.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: A note indicates that class has been moved to the science lab. The lab is unoccupied, but Jan Di waits, not realizing that the room is filling with fumes.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Jan Di's father gets kidnapped by loan sharks, which inevitably forces Jan Di's mother to swallow her pride and plead for Madam Kang's help.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: The F4 guys have moved on to college while Jan Di has entered her senior year of high school. She has not heard from Jun Pyo since he left.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Jan Di waits to hear the conclusions of F3's meeting with Jun-pyo, and can tell from their look that it was not good.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: After treating Jan Di in a cruel manner, Mr. Jung prepares everything so that Jun-Pyo can visit her in Seoul. Will he be able to go without Madam Kang finding out?

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Jan Di's family hears about Jun-pyo's engagement and asks her miserably if it's true.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Ji-hoo can see how hurt Jan Di is by the kiss, and continues to follow her as she stumbles around in shock.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Jun-pyo begins seeing Jae-Kyung in a new light, like he's seeing her for the first time as a potential friend -- or maybe a kindred spirit.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Woo-bin tries to talk some sense into Yi-jung, who is determined to drink himself into a stupor.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: At home, Jun-hee reminisces about how her mother meddled in her life, in much the same way she's interfering in Jun-pyo's now.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Yi-jung is waiting outside for Eun-jae, excited to see her -- until he listens in on her phone conversation; from her words, it sounds like a call to a boyfriend.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Jun-pyo begs for Jan Di to advise him not to get married.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Jun-pyo tells Jan Di that he'll protect her, but to Jun-pyo's surprise, she says that she does not want to be cosseted or taken care of.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Perhaps feeling it's time to expose one truth, Mr. Jung leads Jun-hee to the unknown patient's bedside; at the sight of her father, Jun-hee breaks down.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Jun-pyo asks Yumi to make the lunchbox once more, confirming that Yumi had taken credit for her lunch.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Just being rich isn't enough to endure the teen oppression at Shinhwa -- loners are often marked as targets by the bored über-clique called F4.

programme.title: Dancing Cat
programme.desc: A documentary chronicling the daily dramas of Korea's street cats through the eyes of a poet.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: A wicked rumor is spread on the Internet that a second-year student at Shinhwa High School is pregnant.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Not too eager to watch Jan Di dancing with one of his best friends, Jun Pyo exits the ballroom and ends up taking out his frustrations by the poolside.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Making a rash choice, Jan Di tells her mother she has to go somewhere and runs off, heading toward Namsan Tower.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Jan Di's bike is set ablaze while she is attacked with water balloons and blasted with a fire extinguisher.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: A psychic foresees that Jan Di will be losing something significant.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Jun Pyo intends to expel his best friend and Jan Di out of Shinhwa High School -- and out of F4.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Jan Di dashes to the hospital after being informed that Jun Pyo had been in an accident.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Yi-jung gets a frantic call from his mother threatening to commit suicide because of his father.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Yi Jung's mother frantically calls him threatening to commit suicide.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: A note indicates that class has been moved to the science lab. The lab is unoccupied, but Jan Di waits, not realizing that the room is filling with fumes.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Jan Di's father gets kidnapped by loan sharks, which inevitably forces Jan Di's mother to swallow her pride and plead for Madam Kang's help.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: The F4 guys have moved on to college while Jan Di has entered her senior year of high school. She has not heard from Jun Pyo since he left.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Jan Di waits to hear the conclusions of F3's meeting with Jun-pyo, and can tell from their look that it was not good.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: After treating Jan Di in a cruel manner, Mr. Jung prepares everything so that Jun-Pyo can visit her in Seoul. Will he be able to go without Madam Kang finding out?

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Jan Di's family hears about Jun-pyo's engagement and asks her miserably if it's true.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Ji-hoo can see how hurt Jan Di is by the kiss, and continues to follow her as she stumbles around in shock.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Jun-pyo begins seeing Jae-Kyung in a new light, like he's seeing her for the first time as a potential friend -- or maybe a kindred spirit.

programme.title: Hello, Goodbye
programme.desc: Aoi and Hazuka are classmates, but they are not friends. The two girls meet an elderly woman who wants to give a love letter to her first love. Hazuka and Aoi try to find her first love.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Woo-bin tries to talk some sense into Yi-jung, who is determined to drink himself into a stupor.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: At home, Jun-hee reminisces about how her mother meddled in her life, in much the same way she's interfering in Jun-pyo's now.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Yi-jung is waiting outside for Eun-jae, excited to see her -- until he listens in on her phone conversation; from her words, it sounds like a call to a boyfriend.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Jun-pyo begs for Jan Di to advise him not to get married.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Jun-pyo tells Jan Di that he'll protect her, but to Jun-pyo's surprise, she says that she does not want to be cosseted or taken care of.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Perhaps feeling it's time to expose one truth, Mr. Jung leads Jun-hee to the unknown patient's bedside; at the sight of her father, Jun-hee breaks down.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Jun-pyo asks Yumi to make the lunchbox once more, confirming that Yumi had taken credit for her lunch.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Just being rich isn't enough to endure the teen oppression at Shinhwa -- loners are often marked as targets by the bored über-clique called F4.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: A wicked rumor is spread on the Internet that a second-year student at Shinhwa High School is pregnant.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Not too eager to watch Jan Di dancing with one of his best friends, Jun Pyo exits the ballroom and ends up taking out his frustrations by the poolside.

programme.title: Boys Over Flowers
programme.desc: Making a rash choice, Jan Di tells her mother she has to go somewhere and runs off, heading toward Namsan Tower.

programme.desc: Emily Chang meets Anthropic co-founders Dario and Daniela Amodei for a rare, in-depth discussion of the startup's origin story, its battles with the Pentagon and how the company says it intends to put safety first in the high-stakes AI race.

programme.title: The Circuit
programme.sub-title: Anthropic Co-Founders Dario and Daniela Amodei (Part 2)
programme.desc: The host sits down with Anthropic co-founders Dario and Daniela Amodei to discuss the firm's origin, its friction with the Pentagon, and plans to prioritize AI race safety.

programme.title: The Circuit
programme.desc: Meghan, the Duchess of Sussex, opens up in a wide-ranging interview with Emily Chang about life beyond the royal spotlight and her latest ventures in business and entertainment.

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: An emotional documentary that will touch the heart of audiences around the world.; four female climbers face the sporting challenge of a lifetime as they attempt to compete in the first ever Olympic climbing competition at Tokyo 2020.

programme.title: Bloomberg Investigates
programme.sub-title: How To Spot A Russian Deepfake
programme.desc: Russia-based groups have been found to be spreading disinformation for decades; now a new one is capitalizing on AI to more effectively sow doubt and chaos.

programme.title: Bloomberg Investigates
programme.sub-title: Inside the Legal Fight Against Trump's Deportation Campaign
programme.desc: Spending a year following a group of lawyers as they sue the Department of Homeland Security and scramble to help free those arrested under Donald Trump's immigration policies.

programme.title: Bloomberg Investigates
programme.desc: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission is supposed to root out graft; instead, some of its members have been accused of perpetuating the very wrongdoing they were meant to stop.

programme.title: Bloomberg Investigates
programme.sub-title: The Making of a Chinese Spy
programme.desc: Ji Chaoqun was a young engineer from Beijing heading to the US to study; then China's spy agency came calling with bags of cash, the promise of glory and an offer that would change his life forever.

programme.title: Bloomberg Investigates
programme.sub-title: The Deadly Quest for Gold in South Africa
programme.desc: Gold built South Africa, but mining kills some who dig for it; in Stilfontein, a disaster that trapped miners underground reveals how a quest for gold became a story of hunger, survival and death.

programme.title: Bloomberg Investigates
programme.sub-title: The Scandal That Rocked the Philippines
programme.desc: A police raid in a small Philippine town reveals a young mayor's alleged links to a $2 billion Singapore money laundering scandal and Chinese espionage.

programme.title: Bloomberg Investigates
programme.sub-title: The Zombie Debts Making Wall Street Rich
programme.desc: A police raid in a small Philippine town reveals a young mayor's alleged ties to a $2 billion Singapore money laundering scandal and Chinese espionage.

programme.title: Bloomberg Investigates
programme.sub-title: North Korea's Hidden Remote IT Scheme
programme.desc: How an Arizona woman’s house became a gateway for North Korea’s IT operatives, an underground laptop farm that infiltrated US companies and pumped millions to the regime.

programme.title: Bloomberg Primer
programme.sub-title: The Race to Challenge China's EV Lead
programme.desc: Chinese companies dominate the EV battery supply chain and are expanding rapidly outside their home market. Meanwhile, competitors are looking to new chemistries to leapfrog the leading tech.

programme.title: Bloomberg Primer
programme.sub-title: The Race to Merge AI With Humanoid Robots
programme.desc: Humanoid robots that use AI are going mainstream, but they're far from proven; with billions of dollars invested, can they deliver real-world value, or will they fall short of the hype?

programme.title: Bloomberg Primer
programme.sub-title: AI Is Pushing Chipmaking to Its Limits
programme.desc: Semiconductors power modern technology, from AI to everyday devices; global tensions and rising demand are forcing the chip supply chain to adapt.

programme.title: Bloomberg Primer
programme.sub-title: The Race to Fuel a Nuclear Energy Comeback
programme.desc: Nuclear power is attempting a comeback, but producing the necessary fuel is complex. From mining to enrichment, can new technologies and supply chains scale to power the next generation of reactors?

programme.title: Bloomberg Primer
programme.sub-title: The Business of Adapting to Flood Risk
programme.desc: Scientists are developing more powerful magnets to enable clean energy sources like fusion; China's dominance of the supply chain for rare earth magnets threatens their global availability.

programme.title: Bloomberg Primer
programme.sub-title: Transforming the Electrical Grid to Power AI
programme.desc: AI's rapid growth is pushing global energy demand skyward, forcing electrical grids to expand and modernize. Now, companies from China to Nigeria are investing in new tech to power the future.

programme.title: Bloomberg Primer
programme.sub-title: Why Money Is Flowing Into Geoengineering
programme.desc: Scientists and entrepreneurs are testing radical ideas like solar radiation management and ocean fertilization; they are finding funding for them.

programme.title: Bloomberg Primer
programme.sub-title: How Flying Cars Became a Billion-Dollar Bet
programme.desc: The idea of flying cars has largely remained science fiction, until now; a new generation of electrified aircraft is attracting the attention of investors and venture capitalists around the world.

programme.title: Bloomberg Primer
programme.sub-title: Why a Lunar Ecosystem Could Be Worth Billions
programme.desc: Governments and entrepreneurs are racing to commercialize the moon; these efforts could unlock a new, deep-space economy; but to return and stay will require the highest levels of innovation.

programme.title: Bloomberg Horizons Middle East & Africa
programme.desc: A spotlight on the fast-paced growth and major deals happening across the Middle East and Africa, linking the market drivers from overnight in the U.S. and across Asia into the region's trading day, with deep-dive analysis of the top businesses.

programme.title: Bloomberg Daybreak: Europe
programme.desc: From London, essential morning viewing to stay ahead of the day's market trends with news stories from the U.S. and Asia in the lead-up to the opening of European markets.

programme.desc: Based in London, Francine Lacqua speaks with guests about global business, economics, finance and politics.

programme.title: Italian Studies
programme.desc: A mysterious woman wanders the streets of Manhattan in confusion, seemingly unsure of who she is. Finding herself inexplicably drawn to a charismatic teenager, she embarks on an adventure with him through the cityscape and into the unknown.

programme.title: Broken English
programme.desc: After a series of disastrous dates, an unhappy single woman (Parker Posey) meets a potential soul mate in a visiting Frenchman (Melvil Poupaud).

programme.title: Best Man Down
programme.desc: When their best man (Tyler Labine) dies suddenly, two newlyweds (Justin Long, Jess Weixler) must cancel their honeymoon to arrange his funeral.

programme.title: A Taste of Hunger
programme.desc: A couple sacrifice everything to get a coveted Michelin star for their popular restaurant in Copenhagen, Denmark.

programme.title: Experimenter
programme.desc: In 1961, social psychologist Stanley Milgram (Peter Sarsgaard) conducts controversial experiments designed to measure conformity, conscience and free will.

programme.title: Force majeure
programme.desc: A man's selfish reaction to the danger posed by an avalanche causes cracks in his marriage and relationships with his children.

programme.title: Two Lovers
programme.desc: A man (Joaquin Phoenix) juggles relationships with an honest, loving young woman (Vinessa Shaw) and a manipulative neighbor (Gwyneth Paltrow) who has a married boyfriend.

programme.title: Lemon
programme.desc: After Isaac's blind girlfriend walks out on him, his life goes into free fall, and he directs an unhinged staging of Chekhov's "The Seagull," attends the Passover Seder from hell, and attempts to woo a new flame.

programme.title: Person to Person
programme.desc: A film by Dustin Guy Defa.

programme.title: I'm Still Here
programme.desc: Director Casey Affleck follows Joaquin Phoenix as he carries out a plan to retire from acting and concentrate on a new career as a hip-hop musician.

programme.title: Bad Milo!
programme.desc: A stressed-out man (Ken Marino) discovers that a tiny demon lives in his intestine, and that excessive anxiety causes the creature to escape and slaughter the man's tormentors.

programme.title: Borderline
programme.desc: A helplessly romantic sociopath escapes from a mental institution and invades the home of a '90s pop superstar. He just wants to be loved; she just wants to survive.

programme.title: Tucker & Dale vs Evil
programme.desc: Two scruffy pals' (Tyler Labine, Alan Tudyk) backwoods vacation takes a bloody turn when ignorant college students mistake them for a pair of murderous hillbillies.

programme.title: Tim and Eric's Billion Dollar Movie
programme.desc: Would-be filmmakers Tim and Eric squander $1 billion of the sinister Schlaaang Corp.'s money and must figure out a way to pay it back.

programme.title: World's Greatest Dad
programme.desc: A high-school poetry teacher (Robin Williams) is also a single father and dreams of becoming a successful writer, but a freak accident leads to both tragedy and opportunity.

programme.title: Humboldt County
programme.desc: A medical student (Jeremy Strong) who is questioning his future finds a comfortable, if hazy, haven with a clan of marijuana farmers.

programme.title: Nature Calls
programme.desc: After his troops ditch him for a slumber party at the posh home of his rival brother (Johnny Knoxville), a scoutmaster (Patton Oswalt) lures the youths away in the middle of the night for an ill-advised camping trip.

programme.title: What Just Happened?
programme.desc: A movie producer tries to engineer a reconciliation with his ex-wife while dealing with a number of problems surrounding his latest projects.

programme.title: Alone
programme.desc: A cold-blooded killer hunts a widow in the wilderness after she escapes from his remote cabin in the Pacific Northwest.

programme.title: The Two Faces of January
programme.desc: After he kills a detective, a con artist (Viggo Mortensen) and his wife (Kirsten Dunst) must trust a potentially dangerous stranger (Oscar Isaac) to help them get out of Greece.

programme.title: The Life Before Her Eyes
programme.desc: A woman's (Uma Thurman) memories of a violent childhood incident that took the life of her best friend (Eva Amurri) affect her life as an adult.

programme.title: Tyrel
programme.desc: Tyler spirals out of control when he realizes he's the only black person attending a weekend birthday party in a secluded cabin.

programme.title: The Good Doctor
programme.desc: A young physician (Orlando Bloom) becomes dangerously obsessed with a teenage patient (Riley Keough) and falls into an illicit romance. Things quickly spin out of control when an orderly finds out and blackmails the doctor.

programme.title: Marrowbone
programme.desc: A young man and his younger siblings who have concealed the death of their mother to remain together are plagued by a sinister presence in their home.

programme.title: The Perfect Host
programme.desc: A crook on the run cons his way into a dinner party whose host is anything but ordinary.

programme.title: Monsters
programme.desc: A photojournalist must bring his publisher's daughter along when his latest assignment takes him into an area populated by vicious alien beasts.

programme.title: The Ones Below
programme.desc: How well do you know your neighbors?

programme.title: Rabbit Trap
programme.desc: While collecting audio samples, a musician creates a field recording of a mysterious sound never before heard by human ears. As the echoes of music bleed into the surrounding countryside, ancient and malevolent woodland forces are disturbed.

programme.title: Ultrasound
programme.desc: Driving home during a rainstorm, Glen experiences car trouble and gets help from a strange couple that gives him an offer he can't refuse. At the same time, Katie is feeling weighed down by a secret and a medical practitioner questions an experiment.

programme.title: Splinter
programme.desc: A vacationing couple and an escaped convict become trapped by a voracious parasite that devours its victims from the inside out.

programme.title: You Are Not My Mother
programme.desc: In a North Dublin housing estate, Char's mother goes missing. When she returns, Char is determined to uncover the truth of her disappearance and unearth the dark secrets of her family.

programme.title: Held
programme.desc: A couple's ailing marriage gets put to the test when they are held hostage in an isolated vacation rental by an unseen voice that commands their every move and threatens harm if they don't obey.

programme.title: Good Neighbors
programme.desc: Victor is new to Montreal and attempts to make friends with the wheelchair-bound Spencer and the cat-loving Louise, but everybody is on edge with a serial killer terrorizing the neighborhood.

programme.title: Funhouse
programme.desc: Down-and-out backup singer and celebrity ex-husband Kasper is invited to compete in the Funhouse, an online reality show.

programme.title: Hobo With a Shotgun
programme.desc: Hoping for heaven but finding an urban hell, a homeless man (Rutger Hauer) takes aim at a crime boss and the man's sadistic sons (Gregory Smith, Nick Bateman).

programme.title: Censor
programme.desc: A British film censor links a disturbing horror movie to her sister's mysterious disappearance.

programme.title: Let the Right One In
programme.desc: A lonely misfit (Kåre Hedebrant) finds a soul mate in the form of a vampire child (Lina Leandersson) who recently became his neighbor.

programme.title: The Windigo
programme.desc: A Native American teenager and his grandmother resurrect an ancient demon to protect their family from backwoods meth dealers. However, they soon discover that the creature's lust for killing can't be controlled.

programme.title: The Hunter
programme.desc: A shadowy corporation sends a mercenary (Willem Dafoe) to Tasmania to track down a nearly extinct tiger whose genetic code holds the secret to a dangerous weapon.

programme.title: Skin Trade
programme.desc: After his family is murdered, a detective (Dolph Lundgren) tracks the culprit (Ron Perlman) to Thailand and teams up with a Thai agent (Tony Jaa) to bring him to justice and take down his human-trafficking ring.

programme.title: Mutant Chronicles
programme.desc: In the 28th century a soldier (Thomas Jane) and a priest (Ron Perlman) lead a platoon deep underground to destroy a machine that is churning out hundreds of bloodthirsty mutants.

programme.title: Christmas on 5th Avenue
programme.desc: As they decorate a spectacular Manhattan penthouse for Christmas, bubbly Eva Jordan brings writer Lucas Blade some joy for the holidays.

programme.title: McLeod's Daughters
programme.desc: Taylor's mom shows up at Drovers; Taylor is forced to face her problems; Stevie is concerned that her infant son has not smiled yet.

programme.title: McLeod's Daughters
programme.desc: Grace, Jaz, Marcus and Frank drive to the city to present their objections to a minister that is responsible for the dam; Phil tries to patch things up with Moira by bringing her a very personal gift that catches her by surprise.

programme.title: McLeod's Daughters
programme.desc: Jaz's dad, Frank, plans to propose; the minister working with the dam comes to visit; Frank lets his unstable side surface.

programme.title: McLeod's Daughters
programme.desc: Ben's former girlfriend Monique plays on his mind, but he and Marcus are forced to settle their differences and act as a team when they rescue a stolen dog.

programme.title: McLeod's Daughters
programme.desc: Misha, Jaz's former boyfriend, shows up at Drovers with a major surprise; Phil suggests to Moira that they go travelling and start a life of their own.

programme.title: McLeod's Daughters
programme.desc: Tayler has a new study-buddy, Wolfgang; Moira tries to tell Phil that she is pregnant, but is afraid to disappoint him and ruin their life plans.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: Love-struck Jethro decides he wants to marry Chickadee Laverne, a brassy burlesque dancer he brings home.

programme.title: Holiday High School Reunion
programme.desc: Returning home for Christmas, a woman (Rachel Boston) develops an unexpected attraction to her best friend from school.

programme.title: One of a Kind Love
programme.desc: Kyra's dreams of being a jeweler have been put on the back burner, as her demanding job at the Sawyer Country Club is her priority.

programme.title: Carita pintada
programme.desc: Rodrigo se sale con la suya y secuestra a Aurora y a Francoise. Rodrigo, Martín y Eleazar indagan el lugar del rapto. Kimberly trata de seducir a Eleazar.

programme.title: Carita pintada
programme.desc: Diego sabe en dónde esconde Rodrigo a Aurora y Françoise. Hablan con Eleazar, y él monta un operativo, pero Martín y Diego quieren incluirse. A pesar de que su jefe le dijo que desertara del operativo, él desobedece y está empecinado en rescatarlas.

programme.title: La mujer de Judas

programme.title: La mujer de Judas
programme.desc: Altagracia le pide a Romero que haga trámites para sepultar los restos de Simón que localizó porque hizo confesar a Cuca los nombres de los asesinos.

programme.title: La mujer de Judas
programme.desc: Bruno presiona a Altagracia para casarse, pero ella le pide esperar a que Natalia salga vestida de novia para celebrar su boda con Salomón.

programme.title: La mujer de Judas
programme.desc: Casimiro aconseja a Alirio para que conquiste a Natalia, pero sin olvidar lo importante que es ser dueño de la Cervecera.

programme.title: La mujer de Judas
programme.desc: Altagracia le reclama a Alirio el escándalo que hizo en la hacienda.

programme.title: La mujer de Judas
programme.desc: Paulina, conmovida por su dolor, acompaña a Simón, pero él le pide detalles de Altagracia.

programme.title: Mi gorda bella
programme.desc: Valentina esconde su personalidad con Bella de la Rosa. Sin embargo, el secreto no dura mucho, ya que la escuchan cuando le cuenta el secreto a su amiga. Eva es la única que sabe toda la verdad.

programme.title: Mi gorda bella

programme.title: Mi gorda bella

programme.title: Mi gorda bella

programme.title: Mi gorda bella

programme.title: Juana, la virgen
programme.desc: Carlota ha logrado poner la almohada sobre la cara de Juana. Al encontrar a Mauricio en el pasillo, Ana María reconoce que entre él y su hija existe un sentimiento bien fuerte. Juana intenta defenderse de su agresor.

programme.title: Juana, la virgen

programme.title: Juana, la virgen

programme.title: Juana, la virgen

programme.sub-title: Smash Burger and Creamy Chicken with Mushrooms

programme.title: Jamie Oliver: Fast & Simple

programme.title: Jamie Oliver: Fast & Simple
programme.desc: Jamie cooks a speedy midweek chicken tray bake. He shows us cool ways to upgrade salmon, knocks up a shelf-raid quesadilla toastie, and transforms a batch-cook Bolognese into cheesy bread bowls. All finished off with a super simple banoffee tart.

programme.sub-title: Whole Hog BBQ with Pitmaster Pat Martin
programme.desc: Jess teams up with legendary West Tennessee pitmaster and whole hog expert Pat Martin to cook a hog overnight and enjoy a special cut of beef from Australia.

programme.title: Hardcore Carnivore
programme.desc: In windy Canyon, Texas, Jess visits West Texas A&M, where she interacts with a cloned cow, participates in a blind meat tasting experiment, and helps teach us how to break down a carcass into the cuts we see in the grocery store.

programme.title: Hardcore Carnivore
programme.sub-title: Alpine Lamb Ribs from Siddoway Sheep Company
programme.desc: Jess joins the lamb-raising Siddoway family as they begin sorting the animals that are ready for processing. She then returns to the lodge, where she demonstrates how to prepare and grill lamb ribs.

programme.title: Hardcore Carnivore
programme.desc: Jess visits Northstar Bison to learn about the small family-run ranch's breeding and processing operation, then prepares her signature smash burger.

programme.title: Grill'n After Dark
programme.desc: We're grilling our three favorite styles of pizza tonight: Chicago Tavern with Sausage and Hot Giardiniera, New York Ricotta and Hot Honey, and a spicy Detroit Pepperoni.

programme.title: Grill'n After Dark
programme.desc: We're making our late-night breakfast for dinner standards with the Waffle House All-Star, Steak and Eggs, and a Fast Food Sausage Breakfast Sandwich.

programme.title: Grill'n After Dark
programme.desc: Tonight is a trio of backyard burgers! We're making Oklahoma Smashed Onion Burgers, New York Steakhouse Burgers and Fries, and Bougie Castle-Inspired Sliders.

programme.title: Grill'n After Dark
programme.desc: It's sandwich night! We're making our favorite handheld late-night delights: Chicken Parm Hero, (Not a Philly) Cheesesteak, and Bodega Chopped Cheese. We're going to light the grills, pour some drinks, and show you how to make 'em in your backyard.

programme.title: Grill'n After Dark
programme.desc: Tonight is all about chicken comfort-food dishes: a Gochujang Hot Honey Chicken Sandwich, Almost Buffalo Wings, and a Spicy Chicken Salad Wrap. We're going to light the grills, pour some drinks, and show you how to make 'em in your backyard.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.sub-title: Mediterranean Grilled Filets and Salt Crusted Halibut
programme.desc: Andrew Zimmern elevates California halibut, first with grilled fillets on saffron labneh with romesco and roasted cabbage. Then, he demonstrates a classic salt-crusted whole halibut, served with whole roasted cauliflower and a rich cream sauce.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.desc: Chef Andrew Zimmern grills his favorite tender cuts of venison and serves it with coal-roasted sweet potatoes. Then, he cooks a hearty pot of classic Louisiana gumbo using wild duck and grills crispy-skinned duck breast for the topping.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.sub-title: Grilled Eggplant Caponata Toast and Crispy Squab Katsu
programme.desc: Andrew Zimmern creates an elegant, seared pigeon toast crowned with a Sicilian caponata bursting with grilled eggplant, olives, and capers and a crispy pigeon katsu served with a refreshing shaved cabbage salad and punchy ginger-carrot vinaigrette.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.desc: Andrew Zimmern showcases quail two ways: classic Southern fried quail with lemon-basil aioli, and as an elevated topping for a traditional open-fire paella, complete with crispy socarrat. He proves this small bird makes an extraordinary dinner.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.sub-title: Wild Boar Feast: Chorizo with Mussels and Whole Roasted Leg
programme.desc: Wild boar takes center stage! Andrew makes chorizo with mussels, preserved lemon & white wine. Then, he slow-roasts a whole leg over fire, served Southern-style with pickled cherries, glazed onions & mustard sauce.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.desc: Andrew Zimmern champions the often-overlooked mackerel with a main salad of pickled filets, charred tomatoes, grilled frisée, and sweet summer peaches. Then, he grills it whole seasoned with caramelized onions, rutabaga mash and charred grapes.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.sub-title: Striped Bass Crudo and Smoky Summer Succotash
programme.desc: Chef Andrew Zimmern celebrates summer's bounty with an elegant bass crudo and a whole roasted striped bass topped with a herb-packed compound butter and served alongside a smoky corn succotash studded with charred poblanos.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.desc: Chef Andrew Zimmern prepares Carne Adovada.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen
programme.desc: Chef Andrew Zimmern prepares a traditional Northern Italian-style wild boar ragù. Paired with a hearty bowl of pasta. Next, chef Zimmern blackens beautiful crimson filets of steelhead in a screaming hot cast iron pan.

programme.title: Andrew Zimmern's Wild Game Kitchen

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.desc: Frankie teaches us how to use whole vegetables, from root-to-stem, which means no food goes to waste. Learn how to use those leafy green tops you thought were for decoration. Or vegetable stalks you would normally throw away.

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.desc: Eggplant: A fruit you didn't know was a fruit. Often underrated and overlooked, Frankie shows us how to shop for and cook this filling, delicious, cost-effective, and versatile ingredient. Master these cooking techniques.

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.sub-title: Meat: More Bang for Your Buck
programme.desc: Frankie shows which cuts of meat provide the most bang for a consumer's buck, and equally important, how to cook them for optimal flavor.

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.desc: Frankie busts the myth that eating plant-based is expensive. Preparing these good-for-you meals is all about eating seasonally to save money on produce, and shopping the bulk aisle for even more savings on plant-based pantry items.

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.desc: Frankie figured out the weekly budget grocery equation so you don't have to. Breakfast, lunch, and dinner for seven days, for a family of four, for under $150. All it takes is a little planning, meal prep, and using up leftovers in inventive ways.

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.desc: Cheese can be really expensive. Frankie teaches us how to cook with more affordable cheese swaps than what might normally be called for in a recipe. More importantly, we learn how to stretch cheese in a dish so it can be the star.

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.desc: Tomatoes are one of the most produced fruits, especially when in season. Frankie teaches us how to prepare them fresh in a variety of ways. And for even more savings, Frankie will share a few tips on how to preserve tomatoes.

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.desc: Date night doesn't have to be at an over-priced, mediocre restaurant. Frankie prepares an affordable and delicious sushi date night at home, filled with three courses, plus tips on how to set date night ambiance at home on a struggle budget.

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.desc: A big way to save money on a struggle budget is to prepare homemade lunches for work. Frankie shows us how to make five delicious lunches for the whole work week. Each one flavored differently during meal prep so you don't get bored.

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.desc: Flour has the superpower to transform into an endless variety of meals, and a little goes a long way. Frankie teaches us how to make three different meals using flour as the affordable vehicle. Save money, learn new techniques.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver's Feasts for Less
programme.sub-title: Hearty Chicken Pot Pie and French Toast Crumpets with Bacon
programme.desc: From a cosy chicken hotpot with braised cabbage to cheesy French-toast crumpets with smashed peas and bacon, a quick, aromatic fish curry, and a golden pear & almond cake with orange drizzle, Jamie proves simple ingredients really can shine.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver's Feasts for Less
programme.sub-title: Caprese Chicken Burgers and Pork and Fennel Ragu
programme.desc: From crispy, herby Caprese chicken burgers to golden spinach pies, creamy pork & fennel ragù with fresh pappardelle, and a comforting tinned peach cobbler with fruit-rippled cream, Jamie proves budget-friendly cooking can be utterly delicious.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver's Feasts for Less
programme.sub-title: Homemade Bagels with Egg Salad and Cherry Chocolate Mousse
programme.desc: Jamie's Thai red chicken & squash traybake uses clever tips to get more for your money, a sardine puttanesca proves budget doesn't mean basic, a bagel recipe creates a low-cost lunch and his cherry chocolate mousse is a little bit of low-cost luxury.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver's Feasts for Less
programme.sub-title: Creamy Broccoli & Blue Cheese Gnocchi and Beef & Potato Pasties
programme.desc: With inspiration to make everyday ingredients into feasts for less, Jamie shares his version of beef & potato pasties, a homemade gnocchi using leftover cheese, fish cakes made from the store cupboard and a retro dessert brought bang up to date.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver's Feasts for Less
programme.sub-title: Silky Eggplant Noodles and Nostalgic Custard Tart
programme.desc: Jamie makes an epic toad in the hole with onion gravy, silky aubergine noodles with peanuts and mint, a comforting and thrifty chicken, sausage & white bean bake with cabbage cake, and a nostalgic jam treacle tart with old-school custard.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver's Feasts for Less
programme.sub-title: Gnarly Celeriac Skewers and Cozy Tomato Soup
programme.desc: Jamie wraps up the series with a gnarly celeriac skewer & flatbread feast, a hearty spiced cauliflower bake complete with edible lid, a steaming winter chilli that's good for your health and for your wallet and two delicious ways to elevate lunch.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver's 5 Ingredient Mediterranean Meals
programme.desc: Jamie hacks his oven into a rotisserie for crispy chicken and chips and bakes a savory spinach and feta pie. Then, the Godfather of Spanish cuisine, Jose Pizarro, shares some gorgeous Mediterranean flavours using just 5 everyday ingredients.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver's 5 Ingredient Mediterranean Meals
programme.sub-title: Pantry Pasta and Rice Pudding Pops
programme.desc: Jamie raids his pantry for a speedy veggie carbonara and rice pudding popsicles.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver's 5 Ingredient Mediterranean Meals
programme.desc: Jamie turns frozen fish and veggies into a time saving supper. Then, Italian chef, Gennaro Contaldo rustles up some delicious homemade gnocchi using just 5 easy ingredients.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver's 5 Ingredient Mediterranean Meals
programme.desc: Jamie's dressing a salad with a crown of cheese! He's also got a speedy twist on Sunday roast chicken and an elegant apple tart all with 5 fabulous ingredients.

programme.title: Alan Cumming's Paradise Homes
programme.desc: Alan escapes to an extraordinary waterside fantasy retreat, a sun-soaked property with endless skylights, and the ultimate floating villa.

programme.title: Alan Cumming's Paradise Homes

programme.title: Dream Builds on Wheels

programme.desc: Dave and Jorge love art and entertaining, but with tenants in their basement and no creative space inside, Brian and Sarah turn their backyard into a creative party lounge and build a shipping container-inspired structure for an industrial feel.

programme.title: Backyard Builds
programme.desc: H and her tenant Emma are bursting out of their 3-unit home. To help out both landlord and tenants, Brian and Sarah add storage space, plus a common lounge space in the backyard, with some urban inspired design touches.

programme.title: Backyard Builds
programme.desc: Ali and Hana have two very talented young children - and a very noisy and crowded house. To give everyone in this family their own space, Brian and Sarah turn an old garage into a kids' music and arts hangout.

programme.title: Backyard Builds
programme.desc: Brian and Sarah build a sun deck, outdoor dining area and a Grecian inspired, 3-season garage conversion.

programme.title: Backyard Builds
programme.desc: Matt, Mary and their 2 kids don't use their backyard because the neighboring ravine has taken over. Brian and Sarah face environmental challenges to create a beautiful, entertaining and play space for the whole family to enjoy.

programme.title: Backyard Builds
programme.desc: Janet's love for her nine grandkids is endless. But so is the chaos they bring inside her home when they come visit. To take that energy outside, Brian and Sarah build a fun kid-zone in the backyard, but also a sophisticated adult area for Janet.

programme.title: Backyard Builds
programme.desc: Aarati & Dave both work from home, forcing their living room to be a triple duty working/living/children's play area. Brian and Sarah create a flexible structure, install a sleek and sophisticated plunge pool, and create an outdoor fireplace lounge.

programme.title: Backyard Builds
programme.desc: Mom and son duo, Paul and Catharine, are overwhelmed by Paul's multi-level backyard. Paul also finds his current indoor fitness regime frustrating. Brian and Sarah muscle up to the challenge by building a gym in his backyard, alongside a hot tub.

programme.title: Backyard Builds
programme.desc: Andrea and David are foodies, but hosting in their "builder basic" yard is no fun. Brian and Sarah make their farm-to-table dreams come true, with a veggie garden, dining room, BBQ zone, and a play space for their 2 kids.

programme.title: Property Brothers
programme.desc: Tyler and Lindsay love their waterfront lifestyle, but desperately need a family home where they can put down roots. Drew and Jonathan help them find a property in their exclusive neighborhood with minimal compromise.

programme.title: Property Brothers
programme.desc: With three kids and another one on the way, Dave and Lizzy are desperate to get out of their rental and into the home of their dreams. But they've been house hunting for years already, and their due date is fast approaching.

programme.title: Property Brothers
programme.desc: Tennis pros Jessica and Nathan have been living a cramped condo lifestyle for way too long. And now they're desperate to find a sprawling home in the country where they can put down roots and entertain family in style.

programme.title: Property Brothers
programme.desc: Tre and Amanda have been living in a rental for nearly three years, and as the kids get bigger their temporary home is getting smaller. They're ready to find a place of their own and are determined to stay in their exclusive lakefront community.

programme.title: Property Brothers
programme.desc: Kyle and Jackie are living at her mom's until they find a home of their own. And though they've got a healthy budget, they've set their sights on one of the city's priciest neighborhoods.

programme.title: Property Brothers
programme.desc: Tom and TJ moved to Nashville to pursue a dream job, but they haven't yet put down roots and are cramped into a friend's studio apartment until they do.

programme.title: Property Brothers
programme.desc: Juggling after-school activities with their careers, Mark and Eugenie haven't had time for house hunting. While the couple has a luxurious list of must-haves, the Property Brothers warn they'll need to compromise if they want to box up their rental.

programme.title: Property Brothers
programme.desc: Since they sold their downtown condo, Leslie and Tim have been living with her parents. And though they both agree it's time to move into a home of their own, she prefers the city while he wants to live in the burbs.

programme.title: Property Brothers
programme.desc: Andrew and Phil are engaged and homeless. And as they couch-surf their way around the city, these soon-to-be-weds are desperate to find a sprawling downtown home of their own.

programme.title: Property Brothers
programme.desc: Sandi and Phil have lived in their park-filled waterfront neighborhood for years. But with the arrival of their baby boy, they've suddenly outgrown their one-room loft-style apartment, and are searching for an allergy-free forever home.

programme.title: Property Brothers
programme.desc: With twin toddlers, ex-marine Joe and Jessie outgrew their cramped Nashville condo before they moved in. And now the search is on for a farmhouse-style home where they'll have plenty of room to grow.

programme.title: Property Brothers
programme.desc: In order to cut down on his monster commute, George and Jenn want to move closer to family and friends. And while that's something they both agree on, they're miles apart when it comes to design.

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud returns to East Sussex where concrete fanatic Adrian and wife Megan's cutting-edge concrete bunker is now complete. Is this raw, exposed concrete home really the ultimate 21st century country house?

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Carpenter Olaf creates an oasis for himself and partner Fritha on a small slice of land in West Sussex, squeezed by a mainline railway and a busy road. All they're left with is a very small triangle on which to build their future life together.

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: In South Devon, Joe and Claire's family house will be a giant sculpture inspired by the local landscape, 70 meters long and featuring 34 enormous angled zinc shards jutting out in all directions. But it proves astonishingly complicated to build.

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Ex-advertising man Geoff builds a dramatically cantilevered house on a flood plain in Essex. It's one of the challenges of his life: a flood resistant architectural marvel on the coast, designed to survive a one in a thousand-year flood event.

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Bereaved Gretta returns from Malaysia to build a single-story, burnt wood and glass pavilion on the land of her sister Mary and brother-in-law Fernando. Also designed by her nephew Carlos, it's a real family affair, a story of recovery and renewal.

programme.title: Property Brothers
programme.desc: Tre and Amanda have been living in a rental for nearly three years, and as the kids get bigger their temporary home is getting smaller. They're ready to find a place of their own and are determined to stay in their exclusive lakefront community.

programme.title: Property Brothers
programme.desc: Super-planner Carson has been crashing at her best friend Leah's place. But it's time she found a Nashville pad of her own. Carson knows exactly what she wants, but finding a picture-perfect property that meets her high standards could prove tricky.

programme.title: Property Brothers
programme.desc: Confined to a tiny home in a hot neighborhood, Katie, Michael, and their three daughters quickly sold their property. But now they're squeezed into a one-bedroom rental until the Property Brothers can find them their forever home.

programme.title: Property Brothers
programme.desc: Tre and Amanda have been living in a rental for nearly three years, and as the kids get bigger their temporary home is getting smaller. They're ready to find a place of their own and are determined to stay in their exclusive lakefront community.

programme.title: Property Brothers
programme.desc: Busy parents Dorothy and John have been crashing in his parents' basement, and with baby number two on the way they're desperate to find a home of their own. It's a race against time, and the Property Brothers step in with a plan.

programme.title: Property Brothers
programme.desc: Juggling after-school activities with their careers, Mark and Eugenie haven't had time for house hunting. While the couple has a luxurious list of must-haves, the Property Brothers warn they'll need to compromise if they want to box up their rental.

programme.title: Impossible Builds
programme.desc: After years traveling through Europe, Iain and Alyson are ready to put down roots. Their search takes them to Inner Hebridean Island of Skye where they purchase a remote half acre plot, but building a traditional house is a challenge.

programme.title: Impossible Builds
programme.desc: Delaval and Veronica are restoring 450 acres for conservation and funding it by building a treehouse in Swanton Novers Wood. A Scottish firm constructs it in 7 sections to minimize damage to the woodland habitat on delivery.

programme.title: Impossible Builds
programme.desc: Jamie Feilden's 60-acre farm doubles as a charity for urban youth. To fund it, he now plans two prefabricated elliptical cabins on a remote slope. They'll feature timber decks, open-plan living, and green roofs—though weather may delay the build.

programme.title: World's Most Secret Homes
programme.desc: A futuristic villa built on the edge of a lake in Austria. A cabin perched on the edge of a river. A unique beachside house built into the rock, and in rural England a modern mirrored house, designed to blend into and reflect its rural setting.

programme.title: World's Most Secret Homes
programme.desc: A Norwegian mountain cabin that is so remote it is only accessed by helicopter. A family retreat embedded into rocks in the Californian desert. In Brazil, an epic jungle lodge.

programme.title: World's Most Secret Homes
programme.desc: A striking modern home has been built out of flint, and then to Switzerland to see a sleek glass residence next to a scenic lake. Deep In the woods in New York State, a far-from-average tree house that serves as a dream getaway for a family of five.

programme.title: World's Most Secret Homes
programme.desc: Explore a palatial cliff-top villa complete with indoor and outdoor pools. A remote cliff-top home deep in West Virginia and then uncover a retreat built into rocks on the rural outskirts of Stockholm.

programme.title: World's Most Secret Homes
programme.desc: A 300-year-old Scottish ruin that's been brought back to life. In the Sierra Nevada mountain, an angular home built from zinc and glass. In Sardinia a stunning waterfront villa. And in Germany, a wartime bunker that lay abandoned for 70 years.

programme.title: World's Most Secret Homes
programme.desc: Head to the Serra Da Mantiqueira Mountains, Brazil, to explore a 2000 square metre, grid-shaped family home hidden in nature, then visit a wartime bunker on the Isle of Wight in the UK that's been transformed into an ultra modern home.

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Street Food Asia
programme.desc: Luke arrives in Saigon for a breakfast of iced coffee with condensed milk and a vermicelli noodle dish. Later, he visits his favorite Pho restaurant, then cooks up some spicy Chili Crab Claws.

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Street Food Asia
programme.desc: Luke continues his street food journey in Bangkok where he searches for the best Tom Yum soup and entertains the locals with a rare Thai delicacy.

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Street Food Asia
programme.sub-title: Malaysian Flying Noodles and Pork Bone Soup
programme.desc: In Kuala Lumpur, Luke meets the famous Mr Hung with a reputation for flying instant noodles and Mr Sou who cooks a hearty Pork Bone Soup with medicinal herbal properties.

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Street Food Asia
programme.desc: Luke continues his street food adventure exploring Jakarta, a melting pot of diverse cultural cuisines, where he indulges in an Indonesian dessert, the sweet Martabak, known as the "King of Street Food".

programme.title: The Pizza City You've Never Heard Of
programme.desc: George Kalivas goes on a mission to set the record straight about where to find the best pies on the planet.

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Road Trip
programme.desc: On a mission to taste the world's best breakfast, the three big egos Gordon, Gino and Fred are venturing out of their comfort zones crossing over the USA border into Mexico. They hope to stay out of trouble as they drive their bad boy transporter.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver Cooks the Mediterranean
programme.desc: Jamie heads to Marseille in the south of France. Inspired by their stunning seafood, the traditional flavors of Provence, and the multicultural dishes being served up there, he cooks up a steak with a twist and a sublime courgette and olive tart.

programme.title: Pati's Mexican Table
programme.desc: Gualberto "Weber" Elizondo surprised his family when his grilling hobby turned him into a local celebrity and internet sensation. Meanwhile, chef Chuy Villareal of Cara De Vaca wants the world to know that the north of Mexico isn't just carne asada.

programme.title: Pati's Mexican Table
programme.desc: Two sisters made a little shack on the Rio Ramos into a must-visit restaurant known for one dish: cortadillo stew. Down the road, another restaurant uses traditional stews in their famous empalmes.

programme.title: Pati's Mexican Table
programme.desc: Nuevo León is divided between two pro soccer teams: Los Tigres and Los Rayados. Pati tries the food at each stadium with the teams' stars to determine who wins in the battle of tortas vs tacos.

programme.title: Pati's Mexican Table
programme.desc: Irving Quiroz wrote the book on Mexican bread. Pati visits his workshop for his secret to making the perfect campechana, then meets Chuy Elizondo, owner of one of the hottest brunch spots in town.

programme.title: Pati's Mexican Table
programme.desc: The Garza family invites Pati to their vineyard to help the community make piloncillo - and it's hard work! Then, in the magic town of Bustamante, three sisters make traditional bread in adobe ovens.

programme.title: Pati's Mexican Table
programme.desc: Gaby Molinar owns Huerto Urbano, an urban farm dedicated to providing Monterrey with nutritious, diverse local ingredients. And her husband is one of the region's greatest culinary pioneers.

programme.title: Pati's Mexican Table
programme.sub-title: Cantina Culture and the Morning After?
programme.desc: Cantinas have historically been bars where men gather to drink and sing about their broken hearts. Historian David Canales takes Pati to historic locations. Then she tries the best tacos in town.

programme.title: Pati's Mexican Table
programme.sub-title: Texas BBQ in the Orange Belt
programme.desc: Pati visits BBQ champion Luis Rivas to taste his Mexican take on Texas-style BBQ, and he hosts a traditional carne asada at his family's orange grove. Then Pati learns how Glorias candies are made.

programme.title: Pati's Mexican Table
programme.desc: Chef Hugo Guajardo researches the best of Nuevo León's ingredients. He and Pati visit a family-owned restaurant that has perfected the art of drying meat. Then, she tries his famed charred chicken.

programme.title: Pati's Mexican Table
programme.desc: Pati's climber friends take her on an adventure to the Sierra Madre mountains, followed by a quick elote snack. Later, Pati connects with four top chefs for a carne asada with a mountain backdrop.

programme.sub-title: Best of Salina's Land and Sea
programme.desc: Jamie returns to cooking the food he loves the most, getting right to heart of the Italian kitchen.

programme.desc: Fred Sirieix is heading to Bristol for a winter treat in the company of Michel Roux Junior. Michel can't wait to show Fred his favourites, from the perfect French bistro to a festive countryside escape and Jamaican jerk turkey.

programme.title: Extraordinary Places to Eat

programme.title: Extraordinary Places to Eat
programme.desc: Chef Nadiya Hussain shows Fred the culinary sights of Yorkshire including Bettys' iconic tea and cakes, a rural Wensleydale cheese farm and shop, and a small restaurant which serves homemade samosas, Nadiya's favourite spicy Indian snack.

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Road Trip
programme.desc: Gordon, Gino and Fred start their road trip in Gino's homeland of Italy, where Gino needs help to pull off an event that is close to his heart. Gino collects Gordon and Fred in a campervan to begin a road trip they won't be forgetting in a hurry.

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Road Trip
programme.desc: Gordon, Gino and Fred travel to France, where Fred has been asked to co-host an Oyster Festival in the beautiful Arcachon Bay, in just four days' time. Reunited with their campervan, Fred nominates himself as the driver in his homeland.

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Road Trip
programme.desc: The trio heads to Scotland as Gordon prepares to cook a feast for the chief of Clan Ramsay and his friends. Gordon shows the others how to dress like real Scotsmen as they go in search of Scottish beef, hunt for scallops and play some Highland Games.

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Road Trip
programme.desc: Gino & Fred are excited to head to San Francisco for their very own Summer of Love. Gordon tries to embrace the flower power mood, but he's more interested in the 61 Michelin Stars. The boys travel to a Napa Valley vineyard in a hot air balloon.

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Road Trip

programme.title: Jamie Oliver Cooks Italy
programme.desc: Jamie heads for one of the hottest food destinations in Italy, making hand-crafted pasta, a special seafood stew, and the region's answer to paella.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver Cooks Italy
programme.desc: Jamie is taking spaghetti bolognese to the next level and re-creating a mind-blowing Jewish-Italian recipe. There's also beef stew with pappardelle.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver Cooks Italy
programme.desc: Jamie is high up in the Italian Alps cooking some great comfort food, including dumplings, mind-blowing macaroni cheese, and a classic red wine and sausage risotto.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver Cooks Italy
programme.desc: Jamie visits the pizza capital of Italy to learn recipes from the locals and tries acquapazza, fish poached in crazy water.

programme.title: The Pizza City You've Never Heard Of
programme.desc: George Kalivas goes on a mission to set the record straight about where to find the best pies on the planet.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver Cooks the Mediterranean
programme.desc: Jamie heads to Greece, to the island of Skopelos, starting on the mainland in Thessaloniki. Inspired by meze plates and traditional island cuisine he cooks up a smoky aubergine flatbread and a lemony chargrilled chicken with tzatziki and rice.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver Cooks the Mediterranean
programme.desc: Jamie explores Tunisia to meet traditional couscous makers, eat delicious street food, and get invited to cook with local chefs on a beach. Inspired by everything he sees, Jamie cooks crispy prawn parcels and a chicken and merguez sausage stew.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver Cooks the Mediterranean
programme.desc: Jamie heads to Marseille in the south of France. Inspired by their stunning seafood, the traditional flavors of Provence, and the multicultural dishes being served up there, he cooks up a steak with a twist and a sublime courgette and olive tart.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver Cooks the Mediterranean
programme.desc: Jamie's soaking up inspiration in Spain with tapas, flavor-charged rice dishes, and spectacular seafood. He cooks a stunning pork chop with peppers and aioli and a surf and turf skewer of prawns, mushrooms, and local ham.

programme.title: World's Most Incredible Hotels

programme.title: World's Most Incredible Hotels

programme.title: World's Most Incredible Hotels

programme.title: World's Most Incredible Hotels

programme.title: World's Most Incredible Hotels

programme.title: World's Most Incredible Hotels

programme.desc: Capt CA looks back on 20 years of Flats Class TV and picks some of his favorite moments to revisit.

programme.title: The Captain's Log
programme.desc: From Miami to Apalachicola Bay, Capt. Jonathan Moss revisits some of his favorite moments in Florida from this season.

programme.title: Chasin' the Sun
programme.desc: Hosts Justin Leake and Travis Holeman highlight the coastal lifestyle and diverse fishing opportunities in Panama City Beach, Fla.

programme.title: Chasin' the Sun
programme.desc: When tarpon first don't cooperate, it's time for Capt. Justin and friend Ethan Register to have a competition for dinner options in the Gulf's grocery store.

programme.title: Storytellers
programme.desc: Joining John Sewell and Laramie Barrett from Fortified Cottonseed; Laramie gets to see first hand how their product works in the field as John has implemented it 365 days a year.

programme.title: Storytellers
programme.desc: Cable is a huge supporter of Texas Hunting and everything Texas Trophy Hunters.

programme.title: Deer and Deer Hunting
programme.desc: Whether you're hunting 10 acres or 1,000, how you handle boundaries, buck movement, and communication with neighboring hunters can make or break your season. The team shares real-world scenarios and proven strategies to foster cooperation.

programme.title: Winchester Life
programme.desc: Highlighting the stories and people who have made the shooting sports and hunting lifestyle a part of their lives.

programme.title: Hard Truths Of Conservation
programme.desc: A remnant of the last Ice Age, the musk ox is almost mythical in appearance. For the people who share the Barren Ground region of Canada with them, they are an integral part of their culture.

programme.title: Saltwater Experience
programme.desc: Captains Tom Rowland and Rich Tudor use Lowrance Active Target all day to locate fish, first targeting bridge tarpon, followed by unearthing a variety of species in Florida Bay's mullet muds.

programme.title: Saltwater Experience
programme.desc: Captains Tom Rowland and Rich Tudor chase tarpon of all sizes around Florida Bay and throughout the Everglades.

programme.title: Saltwater Experience
programme.desc: Captains Tom Rowland and Rich Tudor catch spotted sea trout and jacks on the grass flats and potholes of Florida Bay.

programme.title: Saltwater Experience
programme.desc: Captains Tom Rowland and Rich Tudor head to the Seven-Mile Bridge in the Florida Keys, targeting big crevalle jacks and permit.

programme.title: Saltwater Experience
programme.desc: Captains Tom Rowland and Rich Tudor head offshore to fish the Marathon Hump, taking advantage of their 26-foot hybrid boat's versatility to find some exciting blackfin and skipjack tuna.

programme.title: Saltwater Experience
programme.desc: Captains Tom Rowland and Rich Tudor are joined by scientist Nick Haddad, who educates the hosts and the fishing world on the various descending devices used to preserve stressed catch from deeper waters.

programme.title: Blair Wiggins Outdoors
programme.desc: Tournament angler Blair Wiggins of "Addictive Fishing" continues his fishing adventures but also shares his love of everything outdoors, including hunting, cooking, beekeeping and more.

programme.title: We Caught That
programme.desc: Capt. Jason Dozier is a full-time fishing and hunting guide passionate about putting friends and clients on anything that can be caught or harvested from or near the water; it's time to showcase these adventures.

programme.title: Local Knowledge
programme.desc: Focusing on the culture of fishing, as seen through the viewpoints of two drastically different fishermen.

programme.title: Local Knowledge
programme.sub-title: Fishing With Zeus in Key West
programme.desc: Rush teams up with Jesus Diaz for a well-earned busman's holiday in the beautiful waters of Key West; the duo maps out a strategic circuit through Key West's surrounding waters to target cobia, grouper, snapper, and tuna for a busy day off work.

programme.title: Making Waves in the Palm Beaches
programme.desc: Featuring the Chase 'n Tailz Fishing Tournament, the North Palm Beach Rowing Club, the Palm Beach Sailing Club, holiday boat parade and the Palm Beach International Boat Show.

programme.title: Wild Palm Beaches
programme.desc: Featuring PBC ERM at the Loxahatchee Slough, The River Center in Jupiter, Delaware Scrub Urban Natural Area, and The Art of Nature segment with Lawrence Leyderman.

programme.title: Reel Animals
programme.desc: Captain Mike and Captain Vincent Sabatella catch some big Grouper in the shallow waters of Citrus County.

programme.title: Reel Animals
programme.desc: Captain Mike Anderson and Captain Sebastian Velez get on a great Trout bite in their home waters.

programme.title: The Captain's Log
programme.desc: From Miami to Apalachicola Bay, Capt. Jonathan Moss revisits some of his favorite moments in Florida from this season.

programme.title: Flats Class
programme.desc: Learning the fundamentals of flats fishing and the advanced tactics used by proven tournament anglers.

programme.title: Local Knowledge
programme.desc: Focusing on the culture of fishing, as seen through the viewpoints of two drastically different fishermen.

programme.title: Local Knowledge
programme.desc: SoCal summer is calling, and the Local Knowledge crew is back on Ali's home turf in San Diego, California. With reports of hot bites and fish moving in, the crew faces a classic Southern California dilemma.

programme.title: Sail Kings
programme.desc: Capt. Jared travels to Venice, Louisiana, where the Mississippi River meets the Gulf, joining Capt. Garrett George and Capt. Jesse LeBoeuf for an action-packed offshore adventure in pursuit of Redfish and Tuna.

programme.title: The Captain's Log
programme.desc: Embark on a thrilling adventure with Captain Jonathan Moss -- a passionate angler, guide, and conservationist. Explore stunning fishing spots as he shares his invaluable tips, tricks, and tales from each journey, creating unforgettable memories.

programme.title: Into the Blue
programme.sub-title: Palm Beach in the New Invincible Catamaran
programme.desc: Captains Scott Walker and Steve Rodger head out to Palm Beach with their friend Captain Dave Fields to break in their new Invincible catamaran on a fishing trip.

programme.title: The Direction
programme.desc: We're joined by Worship Leader, Rob Key. A musical talent that's chased the country music dream, but found his calling as worship leader at Taylorsville Community Church; Rob's love for serving the Lord is his top priority, but he also likes fishing.
